mnemonics.title: AND Truth Table
mnemonics.content: Think of AND as 'All Must Be True' - like needing all fingers up to show approval

mnemonics.title: OR Truth Table
mnemonics.content: Think of OR as 'One or More' - like voting where any yes wins

mnemonics.title: XOR Remember
mnemonics.content: XOR = 'Exclusive OR' - only ONE can be true, not both (like choosing between coffee OR tea, not both)

mnemonics.title: Short-Circuit
mnemonics.content: Short-Circuit: Think of electrical circuit - if current stops flowing (false), rest of circuit doesn't get powered

acronyms.usage: AND is true only when ALL operands are true
OR is true when ONE or more operands are true
XOR excludes the case when both are true

visualTips.visualization: Draw a 2x2 grid with operands as rows and columns, fill cells based on operator logic
Think of a chain reaction - if first link breaks (false for AND), rest doesn't happen
Use a flow chart: Decision diamond (true goes one way, false goes another)
Draw a circle with NOT outside, break the AND/OR inside and add NOT to each term

analogies.analogy: AND is like a door with multiple locks - all locks must be unlocked to open the door
OR is like having multiple keys to open a door - any key works
XOR is like a light switch with two buttons - pressing both does nothing, only one works
NOT is like a mirror - it reflects the opposite image
